title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Frozen Shoulder
intro: Herbal teas for Frozen Shoulder are a natural and holistic approach to alleviate the pain and stiffness associated with this condition, which is characterized by a sudden onset of pain and limited mobility in the shoulder joint. Herbal teas can effectively treat frozen shoulder by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and improving blood circulation, making it an excellent alternative to conventional treatments. Examples of herbal teas that can help treat frozen shoulder include Ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory properties to reduce pain and swelling; Turmeric tea, which contains curcumin that reduces inflammation and promotes healing; Chamomile tea, which calms the mind and body, reducing muscle tension and promoting relaxation; and Epsom salt tea, which helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Additionally, other herbal teas such as Willow Bark tea, which contains salicylic acid that reduces pain and inflammation, and Boswellia tea, which reduces inflammation and improves joint mobility, can also be used to treat frozen shoulder.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been increasingly used to treat frozen shoulder ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa, such as curcuminoids, have been found to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to the development of frozen shoulder. By reducing inflammation and pain, Curcuma longa teas help to improve range of motion and alleviate symptoms associated with frozen shoulder, such as stiffness and limited mobility. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat frozen shoulder include reduced pain and inflammation, improved joint mobility, and a lower risk of chronic shoulder damage, making it a promising natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa powder and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add the Curcuma longa powder to the boiling water and stir well.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool for a few minutes.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve frozen shoulder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecular biology reports", Curcuma longa teas for frozen shoulder may have potential benefits, including suppressing oxidative stress and inhibiting inflammatory cytokines such as NF-kB, IL-6, and TNF-α, which could aid in reducing inflammation and pain associated with frozen shoulder.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, due to its high turmeric content, as well as allergic reactions like skin rashes and itching in rare cases. When using Curcuma longa teas for frozen shoulder treatment, precautions should be taken to avoid excessive consumption, which may lead to stomach upset and interact with other medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, and it's also recommended to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Curcuma longa tea, 2 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of turmeric powder for each cup of tea.
Consume tea for 3-4 weeks to notice improvements in shoulder mobility.
Repeat tea consumption for 2-3 months for optimal relief from frozen shoulder.
Combine Curcuma longa tea with physical therapy and stretching exercises for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizomes of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder ailment, a condition characterized by pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of ginger, attributed to bioactive constituents such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. By increasing blood circulation and reducing muscle spasms, ginger teas alleviate stiffness and promote mobility, enabling patients to regain their range of motion. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat frozen shoulder include reduced pain, improved mobility, and a decrease in the need for surgical intervention, making it a valuable natural remedy for this debilitating condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Place the dried root in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour the boiling water over the Zingiber officinale root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup and discard the root.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, to help relieve frozen shoulder pain and inflammation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder symptoms, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high ginger content, which can irritate the digestive system. To minimize potential side effects, individuals should start with small doses, avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach, and monitor their body's response to adjust their consumption accordingly.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day.
Take 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of Zingiber officinale powder 2-3 times a day.
Continue using Zingiber officinale for 4-6 weeks for best results.
Use heat or cold therapy before or after drinking Zingiber officinale tea.
Combine Zingiber officinale with exercise and physical therapy for faster recovery.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the roots of the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder, a condition characterized by pain and limited mobility in the shoulder joint. The anti-inflammatory properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, particularly the presence of glycyrrhizin, a bioactive constituent, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the affected area. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas help to improve range of motion and reduce stiffness in the shoulder joint, making it easier to perform daily activities. The benefits of using Glycyrrhiza glabra teas to treat frozen shoulder include reduced pain and inflammation, improved mobility, and a faster recovery time, making it a valuable natural remedy for this common condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 1 cup of boiling water.
Add dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to a cup and pour in boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for 1-2 weeks. You can add honey to taste.
Consult with a healthcare professional before using Glycyrrhiza glabra tea for frozen shoulder, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder ailment, but possible side effects may include increased blood pressure, potassium deficiency, and water retention due to its licorice root content, which can lead to symptoms such as headaches, fatigue, and bloating. To minimize these risks, it is essential to consume the tea in moderation, ideally for short periods, and monitor blood pressure and electrolyte levels, while also avoiding concurrent use with other medications that may interact with its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 3 times a day
Use 1 tablespoon of dried root in each cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes and strain the tea
Continue using tea for 2-3 weeks to see improvements
Combine with physical therapy for faster shoulder mobility

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza teas have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder, a condition characterized by stiffness and pain in the shoulder joint. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Salvia miltiorrhiza teas help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area, alleviating the symptoms of frozen shoulder. The bioactive constituents present in Salvia miltiorrhiza teas, including salvianolic acids and tanshinones, have been found to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits in treating frozen shoulder. By reducing pain and inflammation, Salvia miltiorrhiza teas can help to improve shoulder mobility and function, making it a potential natural remedy for frozen shoulder.
preparation_procedure: Gather 10g of dried Salvia miltiorrhiza root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Add 5-7g of dried Salvia miltiorrhiza root to a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried root in the cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day for 2-3 weeks to help relieve frozen shoulder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia miltiorrhiza teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder ailment, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions due to its active compounds such as tanshinones and salvianolic acids. To minimize risks, it is essential to use Salvia miltiorrhiza teas in moderation, monitor blood pressure levels, and avoid combining them with other medications or herbs that may interact, such as blood thinners and certain antidepressants, as excessive consumption may exacerbate the condition or cause unforeseen complications.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Salvia miltiorrhiza tea 2 times a day.
Use 5-7 grams of dried Salvia miltiorrhiza per 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 5-10 minutes, then strain it.
Drink the tea for 2-3 months, adjusting the dosage as needed.
Monitor your shoulder pain and stiffness, stopping if symptoms worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, which include black, green, and white teas, have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder, a condition characterized by pain and limited mobility in the shoulder joint. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of Camellia sinensis teas, attributed to the presence of flavonoids, particularly catechins and theaflavins, help to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area. The bioactive constituents, including L-theanine, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, contribute to the therapeutic benefits of these teas in treating frozen shoulder. Regular consumption of Camellia sinensis teas may help to alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder, such as pain and stiffness, and promote range of motion and recovery.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Camellia sinensis tea leaves, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water to 200°F (93°C) and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Measure out 2 tablespoons of tea leaves and put them in the tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the tea leaves in the water for 3-5 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve frozen shoulder pain and inflammation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used to potentially alleviate symptoms of frozen shoulder, but some individuals may experience side effects such as dizziness, headaches, or gastrointestinal issues due to its stimulating properties. Precautions should be taken when using Camellia sinensis teas to treat frozen shoulder, including consuming it in moderation, avoiding excessive caffeine intake, and being mindful of individual sensitivity to its active compounds.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Camellia sinensis tea twice a day for 2 weeks.
Add 1 tablespoon of turmeric and 1 teaspoon of ginger to tea for extra benefits.
Take 1 cup of warm Camellia sinensis tea before exercise or stretching for relief.
Use 1 cup of cold Camellia sinensis tea as a compress on the affected shoulder 3 times a day.
Continue drinking 1 cup of Camellia sinensis tea daily for 2 months to maintain relief.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce pain and swelling in the affected shoulder. The bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol and cineole, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which contribute to their therapeutic effects on frozen shoulder. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Eucalyptus globulus teas can help to improve range of motion and alleviate the stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus teas has been reported to provide relief from frozen shoulder symptoms, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for this common condition.

Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Eucalyptus leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve frozen shoulder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat the frozen shoulder ailment, but possible side effects include gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications such as blood thinners. To minimize potential side effects, it's essential to start with small doses, monitor body response, and avoid consuming the tea on an empty stomach or when experiencing digestive issues.

Take tea 3-4 times a day for 7-10 days
Use 2-3 teaspoons of dried leaves per cup of boiling water
Steep for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce pain and improve range of motion. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba, such as quercetin and bilobalide, are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects, including reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation. Drinking Ginkgo biloba tea may help to alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder by reducing stiffness and pain, promoting relaxation, and improving joint mobility. The benefits of using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat frozen shoulder include reduced reliance on pain medication, improved joint function, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves, 2 cups of boiling water, and a tea strainer.
Measure 2 teaspoons of dried leaves or 1 tablespoon of fresh leaves into the strainer.
Pour boiling water over the leaves in the strainer and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder, but possible side effects may include dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its blood-thinning properties and interactions with certain medications. To use Ginkgo biloba teas safely, precautions should be taken, such as starting with a low dose, avoiding long-term use, and being cautious when combining it with other medications like blood thinners, diabetes medications, or antidepressants.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea daily for 2-3 weeks
Take 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves 2-3 times daily
Start with 250-500ml of tea and gradually increase as needed
Consume Ginkgo biloba tea 1-2 hours before or after meals
Continue treatment for 4-6 weeks for optimal results

preparations.herb_desc: Capsicum annuum teas have been explored as a potential treatment for frozen shoulder, a condition characterized by pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of Capsicum annuum teas, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive constituents such as capsaicin, may help to alleviate pain and inflammation associated with frozen shoulder. Capsaicin, a key bioactive constituent of Capsicum annuum teas, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, thereby reducing pain and inflammation in the affected joint. Regular consumption of Capsicum annuum teas may help to improve range of motion and reduce symptoms of frozen shoulder by modulating the body's pain response and reducing inflammation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Capsicum annuum (sweet peppers), 2 cups of boiling water, 1 cup of honey, and a large pot.
Chop the Capsicum annuum into small pieces and add them to the large pot.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the chopped Capsicum annuum, then cover the pot and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a bowl, discarding the solids. Add 1 cup of honey and mix well.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve frozen shoulder pain and inflammation.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Spine, Capsicum annuum teas for frozen shoulder are not mentioned in the provided study, as it focuses on herbal medicines for nonspecific low back pain, but Capsicum frutescens (Cayenne) is mentioned for its topical preparations showing moderate evidence for favorable results against placebo in reducing pain.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Capsicum annuum teas can be used to treat the frozen shoulder ailment, although it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or digestive issues. Precautions should be taken when using this herbal preparation, including not consuming it in excess, being cautious when applying topical forms to open wounds or sensitive areas, and gradually increasing the dosage to minimize potential discomfort.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, a natural herbal remedy, have been found to effectively treat frozen shoulder ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents, including camphor, borneol, and rosmarinic acid, help to reduce inflammation and pain in the affected area, thereby promoting relaxation of the tightened muscles and tendons. These constituents work synergistically to improve blood circulation, reduce stiffness, and enhance the range of motion, ultimately facilitating the recovery process. As a result, Rosmarinus officinalis teas provide a beneficial and non-invasive treatment option for individuals suffering from frozen shoulder, allowing them to regain mobility and functionality without the need for pharmaceutical interventions.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add 2 tablespoons of Rosmarinus officinalis leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1 cup, 2-3 times a day for relief from frozen shoulder.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the provided information, I can't answer as there is no specific mention of the anti-inflammatory effects of Rosmarinus officinalis teas on frozen shoulder.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder, but its use may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, dizziness, and nausea in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive stomachs or allergies to the plant. When using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat frozen shoulder, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with small amounts to assess tolerance, avoiding consumption during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and being mindful of interactions with other medications, as it may enhance the effects of blood thinners and sedatives.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder, a condition characterized by stiffness and pain in the shoulder joint. The anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow tea, primarily due to its bioactive constituents such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation of the muscles. The flavonoids and essential oils present in yarrow tea also exhibit analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which help to alleviate pain and stiffness associated with frozen shoulder. By consuming yarrow tea regularly, individuals can benefit from its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, promoting improved mobility and reduced pain in the affected shoulder joint.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers, or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves and flowers.
Combine the gathered leaves and flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a large cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if needed, to make the tea more palatable.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day, or as directed by a healthcare professional, to help alleviate frozen shoulder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder ailment, but possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To safely use Achillea millefolium teas, one should start with a small dose, monitor their body's reaction, and avoid taking it with other medications that may interact with its active compounds.

The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by reducing pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint, improving range of motion and promoting relaxation of the surrounding muscles. The bioactive constituents of Angelica sinensis teas, including ferulic acid, ligustilide, and butylphthalide, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting inflammation and modulating the immune response. Regular consumption of Angelica sinensis teas has been shown to provide significant relief from frozen shoulder symptoms, improving quality of life and reducing the need for invasive treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Angelica sinensis root and 1 cup of water.
Boil the water in a pot and remove from heat.
Add the dried Angelica sinensis root to the pot and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the root.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.

The herbal preparation's ability to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, such as COX-2 and TNF-alpha, contributes to its therapeutic effects. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat frozen shoulder include reduced pain and stiffness, improved range of motion, and a faster recovery time, making it a promising natural alternative to conventional treatments.

preparations.herb_desc: Paeonia lactiflora teas have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder, a condition characterized by pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of Paeonia lactiflora teas help to reduce pain and swelling, while its antispasmodic properties ease muscle spasms and stiffness. The bioactive constituents of Paeonia lactiflora teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting relaxation. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Paeonia lactiflora teas can help to relieve symptoms of frozen shoulder and improve range of motion.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Paeonia lactiflora roots and 2 cups of water.
Wash the roots and chop them into small pieces. Use a coffee grinder to finely grind 1 tablespoon of the chopped roots.
Combine the ground roots with 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup. Discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from frozen shoulder symptoms. Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of ethnopharmacology, Paeonia lactiflora teas, which have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, may be used as a potential treatment for frozen shoulder due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Paeonia lactiflora teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder, but some people may experience side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset due to its potential interaction with blood thinners and its effect on blood sugar levels. To minimize the risk of side effects, it's essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase the amount, as well as avoid taking Paeonia lactiflora teas on an empty stomach and with certain medications like anticoagulants and diabetes medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder ailment due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which help to reduce pain and stiffness associated with this condition. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, including alkylamides, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, play a crucial role in modulating the immune response and reducing inflammation in the affected joint. By reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, Echinacea purpurea teas help to alleviate the symptoms of frozen shoulder, such as pain and limited mobility. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat frozen shoulder include reduced pain and stiffness, improved range of motion, and enhanced overall quality of life.

Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.

Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, there is no information about Echinacea purpurea teas being used for frozen shoulder, but it does mention that Echinacea should not be used with other known hepatoxic drugs, such as anabolic steroids, amiodarone, methotrexate, and ketoconazole, if used beyond 8 weeks to avoid hepatotoxicity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions such as skin rashes, hives, and itching, as well as gastrointestinal issues like nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. The isoflavones and flavonoids present in Pueraria lobata teas help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected shoulder joint. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the shoulder muscles, Pueraria lobata teas can help to improve mobility and range of motion in individuals suffering from frozen shoulder. The bioactive constituents of Pueraria lobata teas, such as daidzein and genistein, have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them a beneficial herbal preparation for treating frozen shoulder ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Pueraria lobata leaves. You can find them at a local health food store or online.
Use a tea infuser or a large cup to hold 1 tablespoon of Pueraria lobata leaves. Add 1 cup of boiling water.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes. You can let it steep for a longer time if you prefer a stronger tea.
Strain the tea and discard the leaves. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's not necessary.
Drink 2-3 cups of Pueraria lobata tea per day to help reduce pain and inflammation in your frozen shoulder.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Pueraria lobata teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder, but potential side effects may include dizziness, stomach upset, and interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners, due to its active compound isoflavone. preparations.herb_desc: Zanthoxylum bungeanum teas have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder ailments due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These herbal preparations help to treat frozen shoulder by reducing pain and inflammation in the affected joint, allowing for increased mobility and range of motion. The bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum bungeanum, such as ferulic acid and eugenol, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and promoting the release of anti-inflammatory cytokines. Regular consumption of Zanthoxylum bungeanum teas may provide relief from frozen shoulder symptoms, improving the overall quality of life for individuals suffering from this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Zanthoxylum bungeanum leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Steep the Zanthoxylum bungeanum leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zanthoxylum bungeanum teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder ailment, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and increased heart rate in some individuals due to its stimulating properties. To minimize potential side effects, users should be cautious of the herbal preparation's interactions with other medications, monitor their blood pressure regularly, and start with low doses to gradually adjust to its potency.

preparations.herb_desc: Boswellia serrata teas have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder ailment, which is characterized by pain, stiffness, and limited mobility of the shoulder joint. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of Boswellia serrata teas help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, thereby facilitating the recovery process. The bioactive constituents of Boswellia serrata teas, including boswellic acids, acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid (AKBA), and 3-acetyl-11-keto-β-boswellic acid, play a crucial role in modulating the inflammatory response and reducing oxidative stress, which are key factors in the development of frozen shoulder. Regular consumption of Boswellia serrata teas may provide relief from frozen shoulder symptoms, promote healing, and improve range of motion, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Boswellia serrata powder, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Reduce the heat and add the Boswellia serrata powder to the tea infuser.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes and then remove the infuser.
Let the tea cool down and drink 1-2 cups per day for relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Phytotherapy research : PTR, Boswellia serrata teas may be effective for improving pain and physical function associated with frozen shoulder, as it was more effective than both placebo and valdecoxib for improvement of pain and physical function in a study evaluating herbal medications for osteoarthritis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Boswellia serrata teas can be an effective natural remedy for treating frozen shoulder, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and nausea, especially when consumed in high doses or for extended periods. To use Boswellia serrata teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, avoid taking it with other medications or herbal supplements, and be cautious if you have a history of stomach problems or allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Panax ginseng teas have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder ailment, a condition characterized by pain and stiffness in the shoulder joint. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of Panax ginseng teas help to reduce pain and inflammation, making it easier to move the shoulder joint. The bioactive constituents, such as ginsenosides and gintonin, in Panax ginseng teas help to modulate the immune response and reduce oxidative stress, which contribute to the development of frozen shoulder. Regular consumption of Panax ginseng teas may help to improve range of motion, reduce pain, and promote overall recovery from frozen shoulder, making it a potential natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 2 teaspoons of dried Panax ginseng root, and a teapot.
Add the dried Panax ginseng root to a tea infuser or a small muslin bag.
Steep the Panax ginseng root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser or muslin bag.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for relief from frozen shoulder symptoms, ideally before meals.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Panax ginseng teas can be used to treat frozen shoulder, but it may cause side effects such as insomnia, headaches, and increased heart rate due to its stimulating properties. When using Panax ginseng teas to treat frozen shoulder, it is essential to be cautious and not consume them in excess, as this can lead to symptoms like anxiety, jitteriness, and an increased risk of bleeding due to its potential to interact with blood-thinning medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, have been traditionally used to treat frozen shoulder, a condition characterized by limited mobility and pain in the shoulder joint. The anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce pain and inflammation in the affected area, thereby improving mobility and range of motion. The bioactive constituents of Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas, including cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The benefits of using Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas to treat frozen shoulder include reduced pain and inflammation, improved mobility and range of motion, and a reduction in the risk of chronic pain and disability.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of Cinnamomum zeylanicum powder, 1 tablespoon of honey, and a tea infuser.
Boil the water in a pot and add the Cinnamomum zeylanicum powder. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using the tea infuser and discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea and stir well to dissolve.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, 1 hour before or after meals, for relief from frozen shoulder symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas have been studied for various therapeutic applications, but there is no specific information in the given study about its use for frozen shoulder.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinnamomum zeylanicum teas can be used as a potential treatment for frozen shoulder, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high cinnamon oil content. To use this herbal preparation safely, it is recommended to consume it in moderation, avoid consuming it on an empty stomach, and monitor your body's response, as high doses or prolonged use may exacerbate symptoms or interact with other medications.

preparation_study: According to Advanced biomedical research, Curcuma longa teas for frozen shoulder may be beneficial due to the anti-inflammatory properties of curcumin, its chief constituent, which could help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with the condition.
intro_study: According to "Forschende Komplementarmedizin", teas for frozen shoulder may not be directly related to the study mentioned, as it specifically discusses whirl baths and their effects on fibromyalgia patients, but some herbal teas, like valerian tea, have been known to help with relaxation and pain relief, which may be beneficial for individuals with frozen shoulder.
